channel.title: 简报 Jae Daily · 2026年07月06日
channel.description: 国际政治 · 宏观财经 · 前沿科技 · AI 辅助分析

item.title: [未译] Watch: Lyse Doucet on emotion and politics at Tehran funeral of former supreme leader
item.description: The BBC's chief international correspondent reports from Tehran where funeral events are taking place in honour of Iran's former leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

item.title: [未译] Thousands welcome home Cape Verde footballers after stunning World Cup run
item.description: Tens of thousands of fans formed an ocean of blue outside the airport in capital city, Praia.

item.title: [未译] Brides are bringing back the one-night-only bachelorette party: 'You just come, have one perfect night and leave'
item.description: Before they ballooned into multi-day destination getaways, bachelorette parties tended to be one night of raucous fun. Some brides are bringing it back.

item.title: [未译] Ukraine exploits Kremlin’s weaknesses with new weapons, striking key targets in Russia
item.description: The ambitious strategy of Kyiv’s Ministry of Defense, along with the rapid evolution of its arsenal, coincides with Moscow’s shortage of air defenses

item.title: [未译] Years of Warnings About Public Housing Preceded Venezuela’s Earthquakes
item.description: Residents, construction experts and seismologists said for years that Venezuela’s public housing would be vulnerable in a natural disaster.

item.title: [未译] China Frees Pastor Detained in Crackdown on Religion After Trump’s Urging
item.description: Ezra Jin Mingri was released nearly two months after President Trump raised his case with China’s leader, Xi Jinping, during a visit to Beijing.

item.title: [未译] Three things you can do to stop EU border checks at the airport costing you
item.description: Queues are expected at airports this summer owing to EU's new digital border control system.

item.title: [未译] Sara Duterte Faces Impeachment Trial With Philippine Presidential Dream At Stake
item.description: Philippine Vice President Sara Duterte faces a contentious trial in the Senate from Monday, a case that could end her political career or leave her as the top contender to succeed archrival Ferdinand

item.title: [未译] No-gift policy for Taylor Swift, but how much should you give at a wedding?
item.description: Wedding lists are being replaced by cash requests, but guests are divided over how much to give.

item.title: [未译] What could new rights for unmarried couples mean for your money?
item.description: Co-habiting couples in England and Wales could be given more protections if they break up under new government proposals.

item.title: [未译] The Download: a smoking “endgame” and a new Elizabeth Bear story
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. The UK’s generational tobacco ban might not work.

item.title: [未译] ‘The situation is terrible’: aid workers on life in Sudanese city pummelled by drone strikes
item.description: El Obeid becomes key battleground in war between Sudan’s armed forces and their paramilitary enemies, the RSFFatima has lost count of the number of drone attacks on the besieged city of El Obeid in Su

item.title: [未译] Trump’s War Means Higher Global Interest Rates for Years to Come
item.description: Donald Trump’s war against Iran may be over, but the repercussions for global monetary policy are here to stay.

item.title: [未译] Genesis Makes Rival Bid for Vault in $3.9 Billion Deal
item.description: Australian gold miner Genesis Minerals Ltd. has launched a last-minute cash and stock bid for Vault Minerals Ltd. which had already agreed to a deal with Regis Resources Ltd.

item.title: [未译] 【今朝の5本】仕事を始める前に読んでおきたい厳選ニュース
item.description: ホルムズ海峡、鴻海精密工業、4.7兆円の大型上場、消費税引き下げ、原油相場一変

item.title: [未译] Australia Built a Gas Export Empire. Now the Backlash Is Here
item.description: Australia’s liquefied natural gas exporters should be riding high on a A$20 billion ($14 billion) sales windfall from the conflict in the Middle East. Instead, their good fortune has triggered a wave

item.title: [未译] EasyJet reaches 'agreement in principle' over potential takeover
item.description: The low-cost airline had previously rejected four takeover offers from US investment firm Castlelake.

item.title: [未译] 'Start work at 11' - but will other bosses be as flexible over England's 1am match?
item.description: Employers are being urged to use their "common sense" to allow staff to work flexibly where they can.

item.title: [未译] Trump to meet Ukraine’s Zelensky and Syria’s Sharaa during Nato summit in Turkey
item.description: US President Donald Trump plans to meet Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky and Syrian President Ahmad al-Sharaa on Wednesday while attending the Nato summit in Turkey, the White House said. Those

item.title: [未译] Some Lebanese Christian villages 'asked to be annexed to Israel', Netanyahu says
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said on Sunday that some Christian villages in southern Lebanon had asked to be annexed by Israel for protection from Hezbollah, a claim made as he reaffirmed

item.title: [未译] Clay, kilns and the cost of survival for tile makers
item.description: While some firms lean into their heritage, others are modernising in the face of economic pressures.

item.title: [未译] Super Typhoon Bavi makes landfall on US Pacific islands with huge wind gusts
item.description: The storm, with winds of nearly 290km/h (180mph) and gusts of 350km/h, is lashing the island of Rota.

item.title: [未译] 'Definitely felt a big bang': Delta plane hit by firework while landing in Chicago
item.description: The plane had 52 passengers and six crew onboard. No injuries were reported.

item.title: [未译] Moment of destiny for France's Le Pen in verdict to decide her future in presidential race
item.description: Far-right leader Marine Le Pen is appealing against a verdict which found her guilty of misusing EU funds.

item.title: [未译] Australia probes mystery space balls that washed up on beach
item.description: Officials are searching for the origins of six pieces of space debris discovered on Forrest Beach in Queensland.

item.title: [未译] *What Makes a Great Composer?: A Data-Driven Exploration of Music History*
item.description: A very good book, forthcoming, by Karol J. Borowiecki and Marc Lawx, here is the Amazon link, here is the Princeton University Press page. The post *What Makes a Great Composer?: A Data-Driven Exploration of Music History* appeared first on Marginal

item.title: [未译] Thieves steal around €4 million in jewellery from French museum
item.description: Jewellery worth several million euros has been stolen from the Lalique Museum in northeastern France after burglars broke into the site early on Sunday and targeted its jewellery collection. Around 20

item.title: [未译] Why California’s carbon manure math doesn’t add up
item.description: Something stinks in California’s climate policies. Years ago, the state set up a system that pays cattle farmers across the country to turn the methane emitted from cattle manure into natural gas, enc

item.title: [未译] Infuriating Google commercial imagines the founding fathers embracing AI
item.description:
I call BS: the founding fathers definitely would have been Microsoft Teams users. | Image: Google
"Group project, but make it 1776." That's how a new commercial for Google

item.title: [未译] Nearly a Million Investors Lost a Total of $3.8 Billion on Trump Crypto Coin
item.description: A report from a cryptocurrency analytics firm details how those who bought the Trump memecoin have fared, with most retail investors having lost money while sophisticated traders did better.
